skill.name: algolia-search
skill.description: Guidance for implementing Algolia search with React InstantSearch, Next.js rendering, indexing workflows, and relevance tuning.
skill.summary: Implement Algolia search patterns for React, Next.js, indexing, and relevance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged line 3 for a weak cryptographic algorithm and line 52 for system reconnaissance. Both are false positives: line 3 is a search product description, and line 52 warns against an expensive Algolia delete operation. No prompt injection, malicious intent, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: Static finding dismissed: weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged text is the word "search" in the skill description, not a cryptographic primitive or hashing implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is frontmatter metadata describing Algolia search implementation. There is no code, cryptographic call, or weak algorithm usage on this line.

low_findings.title: Static finding dismissed: system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged text advises users to avoid Algolia deleteBy because it is computationally expensive, not to perform host or network reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line appears under indexing best practices and refers to an Algolia API operation. No filesystem, shell, host enumeration, or network scanning behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Search integrations often fail because indexing, rendering, and relevance choices are made separately. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical Algolia patterns for cohesive search implementation.
actual_capabilities: Explains React InstantSearch hooks for search boxes, hits, refinements, pagination, and full state access.
Guides Next.js server-side rendering with InstantSearchNext and initial search state handling.
Compares full reindexing, full record updates, and partial record updates.
Recommends batching strategies for larger Algolia indexing jobs.
Calls out URL synchronization and dynamic rendering considerations for search pages.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or ready-made application code.
Does not configure Algolia credentials, indexes, replicas, or dashboard settings.
Does not cover every Algolia product feature or pricing decision.
Does not replace load testing or relevance testing with production data.

use_cases.title: Add product search to a storefront
use_cases.description: Use React InstantSearch patterns to plan filters, result lists, pagination, and a responsive search box.

use_cases.title: Plan search rendering for Next.js
use_cases.description: Choose server-side rendering, URL routing, and initial state handling for a Next.js search experience.

use_cases.title: Improve indexing operations
use_cases.description: Select a sync strategy that balances cost, freshness, and record update scope.

prompt_templates.title: Start an Algolia UI
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline a basic React InstantSearch page with a search box, hit list, filters, and pagination.

prompt_templates.title: Review a search design
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my Algolia search page design for hooks, widgets, routing, and user experience gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan indexing updates
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to compare full reindexing, full record updates, and partial updates for my data model.

prompt_templates.title: Design Next.js SSR search
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a Next.js Algolia SSR architecture with routing, initial state, freshness, and indexing tradeoffs.

output_examples.input: I need a product search page with filters and pagination.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan covering InstantSearch hooks, filter widgets, hit rendering, pagination behavior, and URL state decisions.

output_examples.input: Our records change often, but only a few fields change each time.
output_examples.output: A recommendation to prefer partial record updates, batch writes, and avoid full reindexing unless schema changes require it.

output_examples.input: We use Next.js and need search pages indexed by search engines.
output_examples.output: A rendering plan using Next.js server-side search support, initial state handling, and routing considerations.

best_practices: Start with user-facing search requirements before selecting widgets or indexing strategy.
Batch indexing work and prefer incremental updates when records change frequently.
Test relevance, filters, and routing with realistic production-like records.
anti_patterns: Using full reindexing for every small record change.
Adding filters without validating that indexed attributes support them.
Ignoring URL synchronization when users need shareable search results.

faq.question: Does this skill install Algolia packages?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and patterns, but it does not run package managers or modify files.

faq.question: Can it help with React InstantSearch?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers common hooks for search input, results, filters, pagination, and full search state access.

faq.question: Does it support Next.js?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for Next.js server-side rendering with InstantSearchNext and initial state handling.

faq.question: Can it choose an indexing strategy?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains full reindexing, full record updates, and partial updates with practical tradeoffs.

faq.question: Does it handle Algolia credentials?
faq.answer: No. You must manage application IDs, API keys, and permissions outside this skill.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains documentation only, and the static findings were false positives.
